Ayahs

إِذَا وَقَعَتِ ٱلْوَاقِعَةُ (۱)
When the Imminent [Hour] befalls (1)
لَيْسَ لِوَقْعَتِهَا كَاذِبَةٌ (۲)
—there is no denying that it will befall— (2)
خَافِضَةٌ رَّافِعَةٌ (۳)
[it will be] lowering and exalting. (3)
إِذَا رُجَّتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ رَجًّا (٤)
When the earth is shaken violently, (4)
وَبُسَّتِ ٱلْجِبَالُ بَسًّا (٥)
and the mountains are shattered into bits (5)
فَكَانَتْ هَبَآءً مُّنۢبَثًّا (٦)
and become scattered dust, (6)

Key Points

Khawf (خفض) means bringing down and lowering, and Raf’ (رفع) means raising up and elevating. Rajj (رجّ) means severe and rapid shaking and being uprooted. Bass (بسّ) means being crushed due to intense pressure. Haba’ (هباء) refers to fine dust, like powder and particles suspended in the air, and Munbath (منبث) means scattered.

Wherever the occurrence of an event or incident is certain, the past tense verb can be used to describe it. Therefore, many verses about the Resurrection (Qiyamah) are expressed using the past tense. «اذا وقعت الواقعة» (When the Event occurs). Just as upon seeing a great flood moving toward a city, one might say: "The flood took the city," even though the flood has not yet reached the city.

The phrase «لیس لوقعتها كاذبة» means that when people see the events preceding the Resurrection, they will believe in it wholeheartedly. Other verses similar to this Ayah appear in the Quran, including: «فلمّا رأو بأسنا قالوا آمنّا باللّه»(2) When they saw Our punishment, they said, "We believe in Allah." «لایؤمنون به حتّى یَرَوا العذاب الالیم»(3) They will not believe in it until they see the painful punishment. «لایزال الّذین كفروا فى مِریة منه حتى تأتیهم الساعة بغتةً»(4) The disbelievers will remain in doubt concerning it until the Hour comes upon them suddenly.

Imam Sajjad عليه السلام said: By Allah! The Event of the Resurrection brings down the enemies of Allah to Hell, Khāfiḍah (خافضة), and raises up the friends (Awliyā’) of Allah to Paradise, Rāfi’ah (رافعة).(5) Yes, true honor and disgrace, high rank and lowliness, will become known on that Day, as the great ones have said: «الفقر و الغنى بعد العرض على اللّه»(6), true poverty and wealth become known after deeds are presented to Allah.

The state of the Earth and the mountains on the eve of the Resurrection

The Earth, which was a cradle and a resting place, «الارض مهادا»(7), and upon whose shoulders we walked and strove for livelihood, «فامشوا فى مناكبها»(8), will become restless and agitated on that Day; an unprecedented and unparalleled earthquake will occur, shaking the Earth violently, such that the Great God calls it tremendous. «انّ زلزلة الساعة شى ء عظیم»(9)

The mountains, despite all their weight and roots deep in the earth, will be uprooted and set in motion, «سیّرت الجبال»(10), and will violently collide with each other, «دُكّتا دَكةً واحدة»(11), until they are crushed and disintegrated, «بُست الجبال بَسّا», and turn into gravel and scattered particles, «هباءً منبثّاً». And those particles, due to pressure, will become fragmented like carded wool, «كالعِهن المنفوش»(12), and scattered like dust in the air. «فكانت هباء منبثّاً» And this is the same story as the beginning of creation: «و هى دخان»(13)

2) Ghafir, 84. 3) Ash-Shu'ara, 201. 4) Al-Hajj, 55. 5) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 6) Ghurar al-Hikam. 7) An-Naba, 6. 8) Al-Mulk, 15. 9) Al-Hajj, 1. 10) An-Naba, 20. 11) Al-Haqqah, 14. 12) Al-Qari'ah, 5. 13) Fussilat, 11.

Messages

1- There is no doubt about the occurrence of the Resurrection and its severe earthquakes. «اذا وقعت» (Idhā and the past tense verb are used in cases where the occurrence is certain.)

2- The Resurrection is the only Event that has no parallel or likeness. «الواقعة»

3- In preaching, when warning about an important matter, we should omit all preliminaries and address the main point directly. «اذا وقعت الواقعة» (Similar to the verses: «الحاقّة»(14), «اذا الشّمس كوّرت»(15))

4- The denial of the Resurrection occurs only in this world, under conditions of comfort and heedlessness; as soon as the signs of the Resurrection become manifest, no one will deny it anymore. «لیس لوقعتها كاذبة»

5- While there is still opportunity, let us believe in the Resurrection, for after its occurrence, the result is nothing but shame. «لیس لوقعتها كاذبة»

6- The Resurrection is the time when illusions collapse and truths are manifested. (The defeat and fall of some, and the victory and ascent of others.) «خافضة رافعة»

7- The earthquake of the Resurrection is extremely severe and great. «اذا رجّت الارض رجّاً»

8- On the eve of the Resurrection, the occurrence of earthquakes and the movement of the earth and mountains are certain. «اذا وقعت... رجت الارض... بُست الجبال... »

14) Al-Haqqah, 1. 15) At-Takwir, 1.

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Waqi'ah

This Surah has ninety-six verses and was revealed in Mecca. One of the names of the Resurrection is Al-Waqi'ah (The Event), the certain occurrence of which is announced in the first verse of this Surah, hence the Surah is named "Al-Waqi'ah." Most verses of this Surah discuss the Resurrection, its conditions and events, and the division of people into those destined for Hell and those for Paradise on that Day. Therefore, reciting this Surah removes a person from heedlessness.

At the approach of the death of Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, Uthman visited him and asked him: "What troubles you, and what do you desire?" He replied: "I am troubled by my sins, and I hope for the mercy of the Lord." Uthman asked: "Is there anything you would like me to grant you?" He said: "No, you deprived me when I needed it; what need do I have now that I am departing the world?" Uthman said: "Then allow me to grant you something so that it remains as an inheritance for your daughters." He said: "They have no need either. For I have taught my daughters something by which they will never be in need. I have instructed them to recite Surah Al-Waqi'ah, for I heard the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله say that whoever recites this Surah every night, poverty and misery will not afflict him."(1)

1) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Ayahs

وَكُنتُمْ أَزْوَٰجًا ثَلَـٰثَةً (۷)
you will be three groups: (7)
فَأَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْمَيْمَنَةِ مَآ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْمَيْمَنَةِ (۸)
The People of the Right Hand—and what are the People of the Right Hand?! (8)
وَأَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْمَشْـَٔمَةِ مَآ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْمَشْـَٔمَةِ (۹)
And the People of the Left Hand—and what are the People of the Left Hand?! (9)
وَٱلسَّـٰبِقُونَ ٱلسَّـٰبِقُونَ (۱٠)
And the Foremost Ones are the foremost ones: (10)
أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلْمُقَرَّبُونَ (۱۱)
they are the ones brought near [to Allah], (11)
فِى جَنَّـٰتِ ٱلنَّعِيمِ (۱۲)
[who will reside] in the gardens of bliss. (12)
ثُلَّةٌ مِّنَ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۱۳)
A multitude from the former [generations] (13)
وَقَلِيلٌ مِّنَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِينَ (۱٤)
and a few from the latter ones. (14)
عَلَىٰ سُرُرٍ مَّوْضُونَةٍ (۱٥)
On brocaded couches (15)
مُّتَّكِـِٔينَ عَلَيْهَا مُتَقَـٰبِلِينَ (۱٦)
reclining on them, face to face. (16)

Key Points

Maimanah (میمنة) derives from Yumn (یُمن), meaning blessing and felicity, and Mash'amah (مشئمة) derives from Shu'm (شؤم), meaning wretchedness. Some have said that the intent of these two words is the right side and the left side. This is because, on the Day of Judgment, the record of the righteous will be given into their right hands, and the record of the wicked will be given into their left hands. Just as the reason for naming Yemen and Sham (Syria/Levant) is that Yemen is located to the right of the Ka'bah, and Sham is located to the left of the Ka'bah.

Thullah (ثُلّة) means a group or congregation, which, based on the context of the Ayah, «و قلیل من الاخرین» (and a few from the later generations), refers to a large congregation.

Surur (سُرر) is the plural of Sarir (سَریر), which refers to a couch or throne that brings joy (surur).

Mawdhoonah (موضونة) means arranged (or interwoven) and studded with jewels. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The couches of the people of Paradise contain interwoven structures of pearls and rubies (16).

The angels are all uniformly believers and submissive to the command: «یفعلون ما یؤمرون» (They do what they are commanded) (17). The jinn are divided into two groups: believers and disbelievers: «و انّا منّا المسلمون و منّا القاسطون» (And among us are those who have submitted [Muslims] and among us are the unjust) (18). But human beings are ultimately divided into three groups: «كنتم ازواجا ثلاثة» (You will be [divided into] three kinds). Just as in Surah Fatir, people are also divided into three categories: «فمنهم ظالم لنفسه و منهم مقتصد و منهم سابق بالخیرات» (Among them is he who wrongs himself, and among them is he who is moderate, and among them is he who is foremost in good deeds) (19). One group consists of the unjust and sinful individuals, one group consists of moderate and average individuals, and one group consists of excellent and distinguished individuals.

In the Noble Qur'an, examples of precedence and surpassing others have been mentioned, including:

Precedence in faith: «سبقونا بالایمان» (They preceded us in faith) (20).

Precedence in spending (in charity) and striving (Jihad): «لایستوى منكم مَن أنفق من قبل الفتح و قاتل اولئك اعظم درجةً من الّذین انفقوا من بعد و قاتلوا» (Not equal among you are those who spent before the conquest [of Makkah] and fought. Those are greater in degree than those who spent afterwards and fought) (21), «و فى ذلك فلیتنافس المتنافسون» (And for that let the competitors compete) (22).

Precedence in good deeds: «فاستبقوا الخیرات» (So compete in good deeds) (23).

Precedence in the promised Divine bounties in Paradise.

Imam Baqir عليه السلام considered the clear manifestation of «السّابقون السّابقون» (The foremost, the foremost) to be the Prophets (24), and Hadrat Ali عليه السلام considered one of its manifestations to be those who hasten toward the five daily prayers (25).

The intent of proximity (qurb) in the Ayah «أولئك المقرّبون» (Those are the ones brought near) is proximity of status, not of dwelling or location. Proximity to Allah is not achieved through mere claims; it has a path, a method, and proof. The Jews used to say: We are the ones brought near to God. «نحن أبناء اللّه و أحباؤه» (We are the children of Allah and His beloved ones) (26). But the Qur'an states: The ones brought near are those who are pioneers in perfections. «و السّابقون السّابقون أولئك المقرّبون» (And the foremost, the foremost—Those are the ones brought near).

In the categorization of the three groups, the name of the Sabiqun (foremost) is mentioned last (in the sequence of the Ayahs), perhaps because their number is smaller, but in terms of honor, they are honored first, followed by the Companions of the Right and others, because their status is higher than others.

Messages

1- The occurrence of the Day of Judgment and the manner of resurrection are so certain that it is spoken of as if it has already occurred in the past. «كنتم أزواجاً ثلاثة»

2- In the method of propagation and the etiquette of discourse, stating the summary of the matter first, followed by its detail, is desirable. «كنتم أزواجاً ثلاثة - فاصحاب - فاصحاب - و السّابقون»

3- Good fortune or misfortune, felicity or wretchedness, is permanent on the Day of Judgment. «فاصحاب المیمنة... فاصحاب المشئمة» (The word As-hab [اصحاب], the plural of Sahib [صاحب], means companion, inseparable associate, or one who is constantly attached).

4- Precedence in faith, action, good deeds, and virtues is valuable. «والسّابقون السّابقون»

5- Those who hasten toward good deeds in this world are given priority in the Hereafter for receiving their reward. «السّابقون...أولئك المقرّبون»

6- Pioneers in good deeds must be held in high esteem (brought near) in society. «أولئك المقرّبون»

7- Paradise is the place of fulfillment and enjoyment for the believer. «جنات النّعیم»

8- Spiritual status is higher than material enjoyment. First, He said: They are brought near to Our presence, «أولئك المقرّبون» then He said: They will be placed in the gardens of Paradise. «فى جنّات النّعیم»

9- Righteous predecessors and pioneers should not be forgotten. «ثلّة من الاولین»

10- To arouse desire and interest, describing the characteristics of Paradise is necessary. (The quality of the couches «موضونة», the manner of sitting «متقابلین»).

11- The manner of sitting for the people of Paradise is in a circle so that individuals face one another and are face-to-face. «متقابلین»

12- Among the etiquette of hospitality is providing a place where the guest feels comfortable. «على سرر... متّكئين علیها» (Upon couches... reclining on them).

Ayahs

يَطُوفُ عَلَيْهِمْ وِلْدَٰنٌ مُّخَلَّدُونَ (۱۷)
They will be waited upon by immortal youths, (17)
بِأَكْوَابٍ وَأَبَارِيقَ وَكَأْسٍ مِّن مَّعِينٍ (۱۸)
with goblets and ewers and a cup of a clear wine, (18)
لَّا يُصَدَّعُونَ عَنْهَا وَلَا يُنزِفُونَ (۱۹)
which causes them neither headache nor stupefaction, (19)
وَفَـٰكِهَةٍ مِّمَّا يَتَخَيَّرُونَ (۲٠)
and such fruits as they prefer (20)
وَلَحْمِ طَيْرٍ مِّمَّا يَشْتَهُونَ (۲۱)
and such flesh of fowls as they desire, (21)
وَحُورٌ عِينٌ (۲۲)
and big-eyed houris (22)
كَأَمْثَـٰلِ ٱللُّؤْلُؤِ ٱلْمَكْنُونِ (۲۳)
like guarded pearls, (23)
جَزَآءًۢ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۲٤)
a reward for what they used to do. (24)

Key Points

«Akwaab» (أكواب) is the plural of «Koob» (كوب), referring to a cup without a handle or spout, similar to modern drinking glasses. «Abaariq» (أباريق) is the plural of «Ibreeq» (إبريق), a vessel that has a spout and a handle, similar to a teapot. «Ka's» (كأس) refers to a glass overflowing with wine or drink, and «Ma'een» (مَعین) means flowing drink/liquid. «Yusadda'oon» (يصدعون) comes from «Sudaa'» (صداع), meaning headache. (The phrase [in Persian]: I will not be a musaddi' to you, means I will not cause trouble for you.) «Yunzifoon» (ينزفون) comes from «Nazf» (نزف), meaning the loss of intellect/reason. Originally, it means the gradual emptying of water from a well. The best description for the women of Paradise is a pearl in its shell, which, despite its beauty and brilliance, is safe from the reach of the unworthy and far from the gaze of strangers (non-mahram). «حور عين كامثال اللؤلؤ المكنون»

Messages

1- The attendants and the method of hospitality for the people of Paradise have specific characteristics:

A: They are available at every moment. «يطوف»

B: They are youths (boys) with beautiful and pleasing appearances. «ولدان»

C: Their pleasing nature is not seasonal or momentary. «مخلّدون»

D: They hold various types of serving vessels. «أكواب، أباريق، كاس»

E: The hospitality begins with liquids and drinks. «كأس من معين»

F: What is offered has no defects (or ill effects). «لايصدّعون... لاينزفون»

G: The provisions are numerous, varied, and chosen by the guest. «فاكهة مما يتخيّرون»

H: The priority is with the drink, then fruit, and then warm and pleasant food. «معين، فاكهة، لحم»

2- Bird meat (white meat) is superior to grazing animal meat (red meat). «لحم طير»

3- Appetite plays a fundamental role in the type of food served. «مما يشتهون»

4- The women of Paradise are those who, despite their beauty, are chaste and pure. «حور عين كامثال اللؤلؤ المكنون»

5- All means of enjoyment are provided in Paradise. «فاكهة... لحم... حور عين»

6- The enjoyments of Paradise are perpetual. «يتخيّرون, يشتهون...» (The present tense verb indicates permanence and continuity.)

7- In the method of propagation and invitation to goodness, we must not neglect the role of deeds in attaining the rewards of the Hereafter. «جزاء بما كانوا يعملون»

Ayahs

لَا يَسْمَعُونَ فِيهَا لَغْوًا وَلَا تَأْثِيمًا (۲٥)
They will not hear therein any vain talk or sinful speech, (25)
إِلَّا قِيلًا سَلَـٰمًا سَلَـٰمًا (۲٦)
but only the watchword, ‘Peace!’ ‘Peace!’ (26)

Key Points

Laghw (لغو) means useless and vain speech, and ta'thīm (تأثیم) means attributing sin to others. In narrations, intellect (‘aql) is described thus: «ما عُبد به الرّحمن و اكتُسب به الجَنان»(27) (It is) that by which man worships Allah and steps onto the path of acquiring Paradise and eternal bliss. If a person reflects that a few days of worshipping Allah in this world leads to all this comfort, ease, and enjoyment in the Hereafter, and yet he is willing to lose all these eternal pleasures for the sake of the fleeting world, this is the height of foolishness. This is the meaning of true loss, about which Allah says: «قل انّ الخاسرین الّذین خسروا أنفسهم»(28) O Prophet! Tell the people: The real losers are those who have lost their own souls. We also read in the supplication (Du‘ā’) of the month of Rajab: «خاب الوافدون على غیرك»(29) O God! Those who turned to other than You have lost and gained no profit.

The people of Paradise receive greetings (salām) from several sources: A) From the Companions of the Right (Aṣḥāb al-Yamīn) (friends in Paradise). «وامّا ان كان من أصحاب الیمین.فسلام لك من أصحاب الیمین»(30) If he is one of the Companions of the Right, (they say to him) "Peace be upon you from the friends." B) From the people of Al-A‘rāf. «و على الاعراف رجال یعرفون كلاّ بسیماهم و نادوا أصحاب الجنة ان سلام علیكم»(31) And on Al-A‘rāf are men who recognize everyone by their marks, and they call out to the people of Paradise, "Peace be upon you." C) From the angels. «و الملائكة یدخلون علیهم من كلّ باب سلام علیكم بما صبرتم»(32) The angels enter upon them from every gate, greeting them with peace because of their patience and steadfastness. D) From Allah. «سلام قولاً من ربّ رحیم»(33) Peace be upon them from the Merciful Lord.

The meaning of «سلاماً سلاماً» is either speech purified of vain and improper talk, or conversations accompanied by peace, reconciliation, and serenity.

27) كافى، ج ۱، ص ۱۱. 28) زمر، ۱۵. 29) مفاتیح الجنان. 30) واقعه، ۹۰ و ۹۱. 31) اعراف، ۴۶. 32) رعد، ۲۳ و ۲۴. 33) یس، ۵۸ .

Messages

1- In Paradise, there are no causes for spiritual or psychological distress. «لایسمعون فیها لغواً...»

2- Usually, worldly enjoyments are mixed with vain talk (laghw) and sin, but in the Hereafter, this is not the case. «لایسمعون...»

3- Paradise is the abode of peace and well-being. «الاّ قیلاً سلاماً سلاماً» (A society where peace and well-being prevail, and where vain and useless talk does not exist, is a heavenly society.)

Ayahs

وَأَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْيَمِينِ مَآ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْيَمِينِ (۲۷)
And the People of the Right Hand—what are the People of the Right Hand?! (27)
فِى سِدْرٍ مَّخْضُودٍ (۲۸)
Amid thornless lote trees (28)
وَطَلْحٍ مَّنضُودٍ (۲۹)
and clustered spathes (29)
وَظِلٍّ مَّمْدُودٍ (۳٠)
and extended shade, (30)
وَمَآءٍ مَّسْكُوبٍ (۳۱)
and ever-flowing water (31)
وَفَـٰكِهَةٍ كَثِيرَةٍ (۳۲)
and abundant fruits, (32)
لَّا مَقْطُوعَةٍ وَلَا مَمْنُوعَةٍ (۳۳)
neither inaccessible, nor forbidden, (33)
وَفُرُشٍ مَّرْفُوعَةٍ (۳٤)
and noble spouses. (34)
إِنَّآ أَنشَأْنَـٰهُنَّ إِنشَآءً (۳٥)
We have created them with a special creation, (35)
فَجَعَلْنَـٰهُنَّ أَبْكَارًا (۳٦)
and made them virgins, (36)
عُرُبًا أَتْرَابًا (۳۷)
loving, of a like age, (37)
لِّأَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلْيَمِينِ (۳۸)
for the People of the Right Hand. (38)
ثُلَّةٌ مِّنَ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۳۹)
A multitude from the former [generations] (39)
وَثُلَّةٌ مِّنَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِينَ (٤٠)
and a multitude from the latter [ones]. (40)

Key Points

Sidr (سِدر) is a tree that has extensive shade. Makhdūd (مخضود) means a plant whose thorns are broken and it is thornless. Manḍūd (منضود) means stacked or piled up. Maskūb (مسكوب) means flowing like a waterfall, and Atrāb (اتراب) means similar and of the same age, like Tarā'ib (ترائب), meaning the ribs of the chest which are similar to one another. Ṭalḥ (طلح), the plural of Ṭalḥah, means the banana tree, as it has also been mentioned with this meaning in a narration from Hadrat Ali عليه السلام. (34) Of course, some commentators consider the meaning of Ṭalḥ to be the Umm Ghaylan tree, which is the acacia tree, known for its very fragrant flowers. (35)

Although Furush (فُرُش), the plural of Firāsh, refers to the ground, mount, mattress, carpet, and throne, and Marfū‘ah (مرفوعة) means precious and valuable, just as the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: In Paradise, carpets of silk in various colors are placed one upon the other. (36) But apparently, the meaning of Furush here is wives and companions (bedmates), and the meaning of Marfū‘ah is their high value due to their intellect, perfection, and beauty, just as we read immediately after it: «انّا أنشأناهنّ اِنشاءً» (Indeed, We have produced them by a [new] creation).

‘Urub (عُرُب), the plural of ‘Arūb, refers to a woman who is cheerful, flirtatious, loving towards her husband, and humble towards him. Aṣḥāb al-Yamīn (اصحاب الیمین - Companions of the Right) are the same people who were named Aṣḥāb al-Maymanah (Companions of Good Fortune) in Ayah 8. Since on the Day of Judgment the record of deeds of the righteous will be given into their right hands, and they have become people of good fortune (yumn) and happiness, they are called Aṣḥāb al-Yamīn. In a narration, we read that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله told Umm Salamah: Allah will transform elderly, white-haired believing women on the Day of Judgment into young, beautiful maidens. (37)

At the end of the reward for the Foremost (the Muqarrabūn), He said: «جزاء بما كانوا یعملون» (A reward for what they used to do). All the rewards and blessings were the recompense for their actions. But in these verses, after the blessings described for the Companions of the Right, the phrase «جزاء بما كانوا» is not mentioned, as if all of this is divine grace (Luṭf).

Worldly blessings have defects; but in Paradise and its blessings, there is no flaw or defect. For example: its plant has thorns, but the plant of Paradise has no thorns. (Makhdūd). Its shade is temporary, but in Paradise, the shade of the trees is permanent. (Ẓill Mamdūd). Worldly fruits are either rare or seasonal, but on the Day of Judgment, the fruits are abundant, permanent, accessible to everyone, and without harm or damage. (Kathīrah, Lā Maqṭū‘ah wa Lā Mamnū‘ah). Wives in this world are sometimes incompatible, but in Paradise, they are compatible in every respect. (Atrābā). Sometimes they are not loving towards their husbands, but in Paradise, all are loving. (‘Urubā). Sometimes they are widows, but in Paradise, all are virgins. (Abkārā). In a word, wives on the Day of Judgment have a new creation. «انشأناهن انشاءً».

Since the past prophets were numerous, their successors were also numerous. Therefore, regarding the number of the Foremost, He said: «ثلّة من الاوّلین و قلیل من الاخرین» (A multitude from the former peoples, and a few from the later peoples). The Foremost among past nations are more numerous than the Foremost of this nation.

Messages

1- The Companions of the Right will have a very high and astonishing status on the Day of Judgment. «و اصحاب الیمین ما اصحاب الیمین»

2- Although scientists have discovered some properties of the Sidr leaf, determining its effect on the air and its surrounding environment requires new research. «فى سدرٍ مخضود»

3- Flowing and pouring water is healthier than still and stagnant water. «ماء مسكوب»

4- Paradise and its blessings have been created and prepared beforehand. «أنشأناهنّ»

5- The virginity and maidenhood of the heavenly wives is continuous and permanent; otherwise, there would be no difference from this world. «فجعلناهنّ ابكاراً»

6- The world has never been devoid of good people. «ثلّة من الاولین»

7- The paths to achieving happiness and attaining proximity to Allah have existed for all human beings throughout all eras. «ثلّة من الاولین و ثلّة من الاخرین»

Ayahs

وَأَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلشِّمَالِ مَآ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلشِّمَالِ (٤۱)
And the People of the Left Hand—what are the People of the Left Hand?! (41)
فِى سَمُومٍ وَحَمِيمٍ (٤۲)
Amid infernal miasma and boiling water (42)
وَظِلٍّ مِّن يَحْمُومٍ (٤۳)
and the shadow of a dense black smoke, (43)
لَّا بَارِدٍ وَلَا كَرِيمٍ (٤٤)
neither cool nor beneficial. (44)
إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَبْلَ ذَٰلِكَ مُتْرَفِينَ (٤٥)
Indeed they had been affluent before this, (45)
وَكَانُوا۟ يُصِرُّونَ عَلَى ٱلْحِنثِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (٤٦)
and they used to persist in the great sin. (46)
وَكَانُوا۟ يَقُولُونَ أَئِذَا مِتْنَا وَكُنَّا تُرَابًا وَعِظَـٰمًا أَءِنَّا لَمَبْعُوثُونَ (٤۷)
And they used to say, ‘What! When we are dead and become dust and bones, shall we be resurrected?! (47)
أَوَءَابَآؤُنَا ٱلْأَوَّلُونَ (٤۸)
And our forefathers too?!’ (48)

Key Points

Samūm is a scorching wind that penetrates the body like poison, and masām refers to the tiny pores of the body. Hamīm means boiling and extremely hot water, and Yaḥmūm is thick black smoke. Mutraf means an individual who enjoys many blessings but becomes afflicted by heedlessness and transgression. Ḥinth means a great sin, and it is mostly used regarding breaking covenants and violating oaths. Yuṣirrūn comes from iṣrār (insistence/persistence). This word appears four times in the Quran and refers to persistence in committing sin.

On the Day of Judgment, the "Companions of the Left" will be in severe torment and torture: scorching wind, boiling water, and black, thick, and burning smoke. (38) «سموم و حمیم و ظل من یحموم»

The art of the Quran is such that it depicts the future and its certain events as if they were the past. It presents the Day of Judgment as if the criminals are present, being tried in court, their files closed, and the verdict issued.

Comparing the People of Paradise and the People of Hell

Here, based on several verses, we compare the status of the Companions of the Right in Paradise and the Companions of the Left in Hell:

For the Companions of the Right: For the Companions of the Left:
Extended and perpetual shade. «فى ظلّ ممدود» Shade of black smoke. «ظلّ من یحموم»
Permitted and continuous fruits. «لامقطوعة و لاممنوعة» Neither cool nor beneficial. «لابارد و لاممنوعة»
Pure drinks. «شراباً طهورا»(39) Water like molten metal. «بماء كالمهل»(40)
Rivers of water. «أنهار من ماء»(41) Longing for water. «افیضوا علینا من الماء...»(42)
Rivers of milk. «أنهار من لبن»(43) Burning and poisonous water. «حمیم و غساق»(44)
Rivers of wine. «أنهار من خمر»(45) Water mixed with pus and blood. «من غِسلین»(46)
Rivers of purified honey. «أنهار من عسل مصفّى»(47) Vile water. «ماء صدید»(48)
Two great Gardens and Paradises. «جنّتان»(49) A confined place. «مكاناً ضیّقا»(50)
Interactions with peace and safety. «الا قیلاً سلاماً سلاما»(51) Cursing and reviling one another. «كلّما دخلت امةّ لعنت اختها»(52)
Entry with respect. «طبتم فادخلوها»(53) Entry with wrath and hatred. «خذوه فغلّوه»(5۴)
Enjoyment of Divine mercy and salutations. «سلام قولاً من ربّ رحیم»(55) Insult and boycott. «قال اخسئوا فیها و لاتكلّمون»(56)
Matchless attendants. «یطوف علیهم ولدان مخلّدون»(57) Wandering between the Fire and the burning water. «یطوفون بینها و بین حمیم آن»(58)

Messages

  1. On the Day of Judgment, a group of people will be wretched, holding their record of deeds in their left hand. «و أصحاب الشّمال»
  2. The condition of the people of Hell is so terrifying that it causes astonishment and wonder. «ما أصحاب الشّمال»
  3. The torments of the Day of Judgment are extremely severe. (Samūm and Hamīm are indefinite [nakirah], which indicates severity and magnitude.)
  4. Let the intoxicated pleasure-seekers know what humiliation, misery, and suffering their heedless enjoyments will lead them to. «مترفین - فى سموم و حمیم و ظل من یحموم»
  5. What makes a sin heavier and more dangerous is persistence in it. «یصرّون على الحنث العظیم»
  6. Allah is Just, and the torment is based on man's own actions. If the transgressors face three types of torture: scorching wind, hot water, and hot shade, it is because they persistently committed three types of transgression. «كانوا... مترفین... كانوا یصرّون على الحنث... كانوا یقولون»
  7. Affluence and luxury are the grounds for committing sin and doubting the Resurrection. «مترفین... یصرّون... ءاذا متنا...»
  8. Deniers of the Resurrection have no proof for their denial; they merely considered it improbable. «ءاذا متنا...»
  9. The danger lies where a person spreads his unfounded doubt in society and becomes a cause for the deviation of others. «كانوا یقولون ءاذا متنا و كنّا ترابا»
  10. Those who sow doubt expand the scope of skepticism. «أوَ آباؤنا الاولون»

Ayahs

قُلْ إِنَّ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ وَٱلْـَٔاخِرِينَ (٤۹)
Say, ‘Indeed the former and latter generations (49)
لَمَجْمُوعُونَ إِلَىٰ مِيقَـٰتِ يَوْمٍ مَّعْلُومٍ (٥٠)
will all be gathered for the tryst of a known day. (50)

Messages

1- Doubts must be answered decisively, even if they stem from malice. «یقولون... قل انّ الاوّلین و الاخرین»

2- The power of Allah in gathering the past and future generations is equal. «انّ الاوّلین و الاخرین لمجموعون»

3- All people will be gathered on a specific day. «یوم معلوم»

4- Although the time of the Resurrection is unknown to us, it is specified to Allah. «یوم معلوم»

Ayahs

ثُمَّ إِنَّكُمْ أَيُّهَا ٱلضَّآلُّونَ ٱلْمُكَذِّبُونَ (٥۱)
Then indeed, you, astray deniers, (51)
لَـَٔاكِلُونَ مِن شَجَرٍ مِّن زَقُّومٍ (٥۲)
will surely eat from the Zaqqum tree (52)
فَمَالِـُٔونَ مِنْهَا ٱلْبُطُونَ (٥۳)
and stuff your bellies with it, (53)
فَشَـٰرِبُونَ عَلَيْهِ مِنَ ٱلْحَمِيمِ (٥٤)
and drink boiling water on top of it, (54)
فَشَـٰرِبُونَ شُرْبَ ٱلْهِيمِ (٥٥)
drinking like thirsty camels.’ (55)
هَـٰذَا نُزُلُهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلدِّينِ (٥٦)
Such will be the hospitality they receive on the Day of Retribution. (56)

Key Points

"Zaqqūm" is the name of a bitter, foul-smelling, and foul-tasting plant whose sap, if it touches the body, causes swelling. This plant is the food of the inhabitants of Hell. "Nuzul" refers to that which is prepared for the preliminary reception of a guest. And "Hīm" is the name of a disease that afflicts camels, causing them to drink water endlessly without being quenched until they die. It also refers to sandy ground that absorbs any water poured into it. Bitter worldly foods are either not eaten by man (which the sinner must eat in the Hereafter: «لآكلون»), or they are eaten sparingly (which in the Hereafter must be eaten until full: «فمالئون منها البطون»), or they are compensated for with other food (whose other food in the Hereafter is boiling water: «من الحمیم»), or they are eaten reluctantly (but on that Day, they will eat like a thirsty camel reaching water: «شرب الهیم»).

Messages

1- The inhabitants of Hell receive the harshest, most humiliating addresses. «أیّها الضّالون المكذّبون»

2- The Companions of the Left are the misguided ones, «أیّها الضّالّون», and we ask Allah in every prayer not to be among them. «و لا الضّالّین»

3- Worse than being misguided is denial (or rejection of the truth), otherwise many misguided people find the success of guidance. «الضالّون المكذّبون»

4- All the psychological humiliation and physical torment constitute the preliminary reception for the Companions of the Left! So, what will the main reception be? «هذا نزلهم»

5- Allah is Just, and His punishments are fair. All hardships are the result of man's own actions, and that Day is the Day of Recompense and Reward. «یوم الدین»

Ayahs

نَحْنُ خَلَقْنَـٰكُمْ فَلَوْلَا تُصَدِّقُونَ (٥۷)
We created you. Then why do you not acknowledge it? (57)
أَفَرَءَيْتُم مَّا تُمْنُونَ (٥۸)
Have you considered the sperm that you emit? (58)
ءَأَنتُمْ تَخْلُقُونَهُۥٓ أَمْ نَحْنُ ٱلْخَـٰلِقُونَ (٥۹)
Is it you who create it, or are We the creator? (59)
نَحْنُ قَدَّرْنَا بَيْنَكُمُ ٱلْمَوْتَ وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمَسْبُوقِينَ (٦٠)
We have ordained death among you, and We are not to be outmaneuvered (60)

Key Points

The previous verses stated that the Companions of the Left (Aṣḥāb ash-Shimāl) doubted the Resurrection (Ma'ād) and asked: If we die and become dust, will we be brought back to life again? From this verse onward, Allah presents examples of His power in this world to remove the notion that the Resurrection is far-fetched. The arguments of the Qur'an transcend time and place; they do not have an expiration date and do not belong to a specific period or location. The sperm in the womb and the seed in the earth are clear and evident to everyone, everywhere and at all times. The concealment of the time of death is wise and entails benefits: on the one hand, it keeps man prepared, as death may arrive at any moment; and on the other hand, it allows for the normal flow of life, because man is not waiting for a specific time for death, otherwise life would become bitter for him. Although some preliminaries of procreation, such as intercourse and the depositing of sperm in the womb, are carried out by man, the fertilization of the sperm and the ovum, its growth through several stages, its preservation, and its transformation into a complete human being with unique characteristics, is solely the work of Allah. «أ أنتم تخلقونه أم نحن الخالقون» (Are you the ones who create it, or are We the Creator?) Even if humanity performs cloning outside the womb, it still does not diminish the Divine power or question His Creatorship, because this cause and effect is by the mighty Hand of Allah, and man plays no role in designing the stages of creation.

Messages

1- Why do you doubt the Resurrection? He who created you once will create you again. «نحن خلقناكم»

2- We should engage in reasoned dialogue with deniers and disbelievers. «نحن خلقناكم»

3- Reproach follows argumentation. First, He says: «نحن خلقناكم», then He reproaches: «فلولا تصدّقون» (Why do you not believe?).

4- In argumentation, we should not suffice with just one reason or example. «أفرأیتم... ءأنتم...»

5- In propagation (Tablīgh) and debate, use the method of questioning to awaken the conscience of the audience and call upon its aid. «أفرأیتم... و أنتم...»

6- Divine power is manifest both in creation, «نحن خلقناكم», and in causing death. «نحن قدّرنا بینكم الموت»

7- Death is not an accident or a chance occurrence; it is based on wisdom, calculation, and measure. «قدرّنا بینكم الموت»

8- The end of life and the time of death are solely in the Hand of Allah. «نحن قدّرنا بینكم الموت»

9- Human death is not a sign of Allah's inability to keep alive, but rather a wise decree (Taqdīr). «قدّرنا بینكم الموت»

10- Divine power is absolute, and no person or power can escape death, overcome this Divine decree, or precede it. «ما نحن بمسبوقین»

Ayahs

عَلَىٰٓ أَن نُّبَدِّلَ أَمْثَـٰلَكُمْ وَنُنشِئَكُمْ فِى مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (٦۱)
from replacing you with your likes and recreating you in [a realm] you do not know. (61)
وَلَقَدْ عَلِمْتُمُ ٱلنَّشْأَةَ ٱلْأُولَىٰ فَلَوْلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ (٦۲)
Certainly you have known the first genesis, then why do you not take admonition? (62)

Messages

1- The philosophy of death is the replacement of individuals by others and the emergence of new transformations. «قدّرنا بینكم الموت... نبدّل امثالكم... ننشئكم...»

2- Allah's power over the exchange of death and life is proof of His power to bring about the Resurrection (Ma'ad). « فلولا تصدّقون... نبدّل أمثالكم»

3- The world is a passage, not a stopping place. «نبدّل أمثالكم»

4- Man is unaware of the state of the Resurrection (Qiyamah)—when it will occur and under what conditions he will be brought forth. «ننشئكم فیما لاتعلمون»

Ayahs

أَفَرَءَيْتُم مَّا تَحْرُثُونَ (٦۳)
Have you considered what you sow? (63)
ءَأَنتُمْ تَزْرَعُونَهُۥٓ أَمْ نَحْنُ ٱلزَّٰرِعُونَ (٦٤)
Is it you who make it grow, or are We the grower? (64)
لَوْ نَشَآءُ لَجَعَلْنَـٰهُ حُطَـٰمًا فَظَلْتُمْ تَفَكَّهُونَ (٦٥)
If We wish, We turn it into chaff, whereat you are left stunned [saying to yourselves,] (65)
إِنَّا لَمُغْرَمُونَ (٦٦)
‘Indeed we have suffered loss! (66)
بَلْ نَحْنُ مَحْرُومُونَ (٦۷)
No, we are deprived!’ (67)

Key Points

«حُطام» (Hutām) means breaking and shattering, and «تفكّهون» (tafakkahūn) has been used to mean eating fruit, telling jokes, and expressing wonder and regret. «مغرمون» (mughramūn) is derived from gharamat (غرامت), meaning paying compensation and suffering loss. If we pay a little attention, the earth turns the human body into dust, but it does not turn the seed into dust. The seed inside the earth has two opposing movements: the stem goes upwards and the root goes downwards. The root absorbs nutrients, and the stem pushes away its obstacles, even if they are hard. One seed turns into several clusters and reproduces. Every kind of change and transformation in nature is the same for Allah. The life or death of creatures, the sweetness or bitterness of water—all are the same before the power of Allah. «خلقناكم... قدّرنا بینكم الموت», «نحن الزارعون... لجعلناه حطاماً», «تشربون... جعلناه اجاجاً» One of the ways to know Allah is to assume the opposite of the current situation, as it has been said: «تعرف الاشیاء باضدادها» (Things are known by their opposites). That is, by imagining the opposite of everything, its position and importance become clear. For example: If the night or the day were prolonged, if the waters sank into the earth, if all the crops turned into thorns, if the waters became bitter, and so on... who could restore them to the current state? Indeed, if man pays attention to the fact that everything he possesses is constantly subject to change and destruction, and if he reflects on the consequences of losing them, he will better appreciate the value of the existing blessings and realize more fully the grace and power of Allah.

Messages

1- The same power that brings forth clusters from a single seed can resurrect a dead person from a single cell. «أفرأیتم ما تحرثون»

2- Natural interactions and processes occur by the Will of Allah. «لو نشاء لجعلناه حطاما»

3- The conscience of some people does not awaken until their harvest turns into thorns and rubbish and they suffer a severe blow (literally: until their head hits a stone). «حُطاماً... بل نحن محرومون»

Ayahs

أَفَرَءَيْتُمُ ٱلْمَآءَ ٱلَّذِى تَشْرَبُونَ (٦۸)
Have you considered the water that you drink? (68)
ءَأَنتُمْ أَنزَلْتُمُوهُ مِنَ ٱلْمُزْنِ أَمْ نَحْنُ ٱلْمُنزِلُونَ (٦۹)
Is it you who bring it down from the rain cloud, or is it We who bring [it] down? (69)
لَوْ نَشَآءُ جَعَلْنَـٰهُ أُجَاجًا فَلَوْلَا تَشْكُرُونَ (۷٠)
If We wish We can make it bitter. Then why do you not give thanks? (70)

Key Points

Muzn refers to white, rain-bearing clouds. Ujaj is salty water accompanied by bitterness. We read in a hadith that whenever the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله drank water, He would say: «الحمدللّه الّذى سقانى عَذباً فُراتاً برحمته و لم یجعله مِلحاً اُجاجاً بذنوبى» (Praise be to Allah Who gave me sweet, pleasant water to drink by His mercy, and did not make it salty and bitter because of my sins). Thanks be to God that by His mercy He gave us pleasant water to drink, and did not make it salty because of our sins.

Attention to the role of water in quenching thirst (tashrabūn), the manner of water's creation (min al-muzn), the descent of rain (naḥnu al-munzilūn), and its taste and flavor (law nashā’u laja‘alnāhu ujājan), compels man to be grateful. (Fa-lawlā tashkurūn).

The continuous favors of Allah are not a sign of His inability to change or transform things. The phrase «لو نشاء» (If We willed) means that We do not make the water salty, but We are able to do so.

Attention to blessings both elevates man's monotheistic belief, (naḥnu al-zāri‘ūn... naḥnu al-munzilūn), revives the spirit of gratitude in him, and removes the improbability of resurrection and the Hereafter.

59) Wasā’il, Vol. 25, p. 257.

Messages

1- The arguments of the Quran are comprehensive, simple, yet profound. A look at water is sufficient to draw man's attention to the Origin (of creation) and the Hereafter. «أفرأیتم الماء الّذى تشربون»

2- The descent of drinkable water is a sign of Divine wisdom and power. «الماء الّذى تشربون»

3- The descent of rain from the sky is beyond human power, and this is sufficient for recognizing God. «ءأنتم انزلتموه من المزن ام نحن المنزلون» (No action is realized without Divine Will.)

4- Creation is a classroom for contemplation, «أفرأیتم...», remembrance, «أفلا تذكّرون», and a foundation for gratitude. «فلولا تشكرون»

Ayahs

أَفَرَءَيْتُمُ ٱلنَّارَ ٱلَّتِى تُورُونَ (۷۱)
Have you considered the fire that you kindle? (71)
ءَأَنتُمْ أَنشَأْتُمْ شَجَرَتَهَآ أَمْ نَحْنُ ٱلْمُنشِـُٔونَ (۷۲)
Was it you who caused its tree to grow, or were We the grower? (72)
نَحْنُ جَعَلْنَـٰهَا تَذْكِرَةً وَمَتَـٰعًا لِّلْمُقْوِينَ (۷۳)
It was We who made it a reminder and a boon for the desert-dwellers. (73)
فَسَبِّحْ بِٱسْمِ رَبِّكَ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (۷٤)
So celebrate the Name of your Lord, the All-supreme. (74)

Key Points

Tūrūn (تورون) is derived from warā (ورى), meaning to bring out something hidden. Warā’ (وراء) means the inner reality or the back of something. The fire hidden in fire-starting tools, which is brought out by striking a spark, is called Īrā’ (ایراء). In Hijaz, there were two types of trees used as fire-starting sticks, similar to the spark produced by striking two flint stones together. Muqwīn (مُقوین) is derived from qawāyah (قوایه), meaning desert. A traveler or wanderer in the desert is called muqwī (مقوى). From Ayah 47, where doubt about the Resurrection (Ma'ad) was raised, up to these verses, Allah has addressed people more than twenty times, inviting them to reflect and contemplate the creation of crops, water, fire, and the creation of man, in order to dispel the unwarranted doubts of the disbelievers. In these verses, the four elements—water, earth, wind, and fire—are mentioned, all of which are under Divine command and serve as means for recognizing Allah and realizing His power. In this Surah, Allah has sought affirmation from people four times, asking whether they played any role in their own creation, the descent of rain, the emergence of plants, or the production of fire. The need for fire in the desert is more pronounced than the need for it in a residential area. A traveler in the desert needs light, heat, and fire to protect himself from the cold, find his way at night, and counter the danger of wild animals.

Messages

1- The same God Who brings fire out from the heart of wood and trees will bring the dead out from the heart of the earth. «أفرأیتم النّار التى تورون»

2- The emergence of fire from a tree can be a reminder of the Resurrection (Qiyamah) in two ways: first, due to the release of stored energy that manifests as a spark and fire; and second, because the fire of this world reminds man of the fire of Hell. «جعلناها تذكرة»

3- Allah mentioned everything necessary to remove the difficulty of accepting the Resurrection, whether it takes effect or not. O Prophet! Your duty is to glorify Allah. «فسبّح باسم ربّك العظیم»

Ayahs

۞ فَلَآ أُقْسِمُ بِمَوَٰقِعِ ٱلنُّجُومِ (۷٥)
I swear by the places where the stars set! (75)
وَإِنَّهُۥ لَقَسَمٌ لَّوْ تَعْلَمُونَ عَظِيمٌ (۷٦)
And indeed it is a great oath, should you know. (76)

Key Points

Nothing and no one can influence the world without the Will of Allah. Therefore, the belief in the influence of stars on human life is nothing more than a superstition, and no one has a lucky star in the sky that causes his happiness or misfortune. Imam Kadhim عليه السلام said: Allah has praised the stars and sworn by their positions.(60) Mawāqi‘ al-Nujūm (مواقع النجوم) refers to the place of settlement and movement of the stars. Swearing by the paths of the celestial spheres is more important than swearing by the spheres themselves, because the grandeur and vastness of the positions of the stars (mawāqi‘ al-nujūm) are thousands of times greater than the spheres.

60) Tafsir Rahnama.

Messages

1- In this Surah, Allah directs human thought from the sperm, the seed, water, and fire, up to the paths of the celestial spheres. «أفرایتم ما تمنون... فلا اقسم بمواقع النجوم»

2- Allah sometimes swears by small things like the fig, «و التین و الزیتون», and sometimes by the galaxy and the paths of the stars. Indeed, for Allah, small and large, straw and mountain, are the same. «فلا اقسم بمواقع النجوم»

3- Every star has its own specific and organized orbit and path of movement. «بمواقع النجوم»

4- Humanity is not aware of all the paths of the spheres, and human astronomy and cosmology, despite its efforts, remain incomplete. «لو تعلمون عظیم»

Ayahs

إِنَّهُۥ لَقُرْءَانٌ كَرِيمٌ (۷۷)
This is indeed a noble Quran, (77)
فِى كِتَـٰبٍ مَّكْنُونٍ (۷۸)
in a guarded Book (78)
لَّا يَمَسُّهُۥٓ إِلَّا ٱلْمُطَهَّرُونَ (۷۹)
—no one touches it except the pure ones— (79)
تَنزِيلٌ مِّن رَّبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۸٠)
sent down gradually from the Lord of all the worlds. (80)

Key Points

Apparently, the meaning of «كتاب مكنون» (the Hidden Book) is the same as the Preserved Tablet (Lawh Mahfuz), because in Surah Al-Buruj it states: «بل هو قرآن مجید فى لوح محفوظ»(61) and in this Surah we read: «انّه لقرآن كریم فى كتاب مكنون».

In the Quran, Allah and everything related to Him are described by the attribute "Karim" (Generous/Noble): Allah is Karim. «ما غرّك بربّك الكریم»(62) The Quran is Karim. «انّه لقرآن كریم»(63) The Messenger of Allah is Karim. «و جاءهم رسولٌ كریم»(64) The intermediary of the revelation (Gabriel) is Karim. «انه لقول رسول كریم»(65) Imam Ali عليه السلام, in Sermon 152 of Nahj al-Balagha, calls the Ahl al-Bayt the "Karā'im al-Qur'an" (The Nobilities/Treasures of the Quran). Man, too, as the noblest of divine creations, is honored. «و لقد كرّمنا بنى آدم»(66)

Reciting the words and understanding the vocabulary of the Quran is possible for everyone, but contact with the spirit of the Quran requires a specific purity (tahara). «لیذهب عنكم الرّجس اهل البیت و یُطهّركم تطهیرا»(67) Therefore, He did not say: "Only the purified (al-Mutahharūn) may recite it or understand it," but rather He said: «لا یَمسّه الاّ المطهرون» (None shall touch it except the purified), and "touching" (tamass) has a specific meaning. As stated in the narration: only those close to the Divine Presence make contact with the Quran.(68)

The Quran is Karim because: Its source is the Preserved Tablet and the Hidden Book. «فى كتابٍ مكنون» Its intermediaries—the angels, the Prophet, and the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام—are all purified. «لا یمسّه الا المطهّرون» And its goal is the education of mankind. «تنزیل من ربّ العالمین»

The Quran is purified, «صُحفاً مطهّرة»(69) and only the pure can touch it. «لا یمسّه الاّ المطهّرون»

The Quran is not as the enemies described it. It is neither the fantasy of the Prophet's mind, «تُملى علیه»(70) nor legend and unreal, «ان هذا الاّ اساطیر الاولین»(71) nor teachings learned from others, «یُعلّمه بشر»(72) nor the work of a like-minded group, «أعانه علیه قوم آخرون»(73), but rather it has been revealed from the Lord of the Worlds for the growth of humanity. «تنزیل من ربّ العالمین»

The Quran is the key to the nobility (karāmah) and honor ('izzah) of the individual and society. Looking at it, reciting it, reflecting upon it, memorizing it, using it for argumentation, and taking heed from it are sources of human growth and nobility. «انّه لقرآن كریم»

In individual, familial, social, economic, political, devotional, military, ethical, doctrinal, educational, and cultural matters, the Quran gives light and illumination to man. «انّه لقرآن كریم»

In the Quran, no weak, ugly, discordant, or illogical word has appeared. «انّه لقرآن كریم»

61) Al-Buruj, 21 - 22. 62) Al-Infitar, 6. 63) Al-Waqi'ah, 77. 64) Ad-Dukhan, 17. 65) At-Takwir, 19. 66) Al-Isra, 70. 67) Al-Ahzab, 33. 68) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur. 69) Al-Bayyinah, 2. 70) Al-Furqan, 5. 71) Al-An'am, 25. 72) An-Nahl, 103. 73) Al-Furqan, 4.

Messages

1- Allah has sworn by the greatest part of creation—the galaxies—for the greatest document of legislation—the Quran. «لا أقسم بمواقع النّجوم... انّه لقرآن كریم»

2- The Quran has placed its knowledge at disposal with utmost nobility, and everyone can benefit from it in some way. «انّه لقرآن كریم»

3- The Quran is not merely words and phrases; it has sublime content that is preserved and hidden with Allah. «فى كتاب مكنون»

4- Benefiting from the Quran has etiquette. Individuals without ablution (wudu') do not have the right to touch it.(74) «لا یمسّه الا المطهرون»

5- The lofty truths of the Quran are not grasped by individuals polluted by polytheism (shirk), disbelief (kufr), hypocrisy (nifaq), and obstinacy ('inad). «لا یمسّه الا المطهرون»

6- The Quran is a manifestation of Allah's Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) and a comprehensive program for the growth and education of man. «تنزیل من ربّ العالمین»

74) Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn.

Ayahs

أَفَبِهَـٰذَا ٱلْحَدِيثِ أَنتُم مُّدْهِنُونَ (۸۱)
What! Do you take lightly this discourse? (81)
وَتَجْعَلُونَ رِزْقَكُمْ أَنَّكُمْ تُكَذِّبُونَ (۸۲)
And make your denial of it your vocation? (82)

Key Points

«مُدهِنون» (mudhinūn) is derived from «دُهن» (duhn), meaning oil. Mudāhanah (compromise/flattery) sometimes means leniency and gentleness, and sometimes means weakness and laxity. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was traveling with a group when they faced a shortage of water, and thirst overcame the people. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله raised His hands in prayer, and rain fell, satisfying everyone's thirst. In the midst of this, one person said: This rain is due to the rising of such-and-such a star (not the prayer of the Prophet)! It was then that this Ayah was revealed: «و تجعلون رزقكم انّكم تكذبون» (And do you make your provision [the subject of] your denial?) and the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Instead of thanking for your provisions, you deny them. (75) Yes, there are always individuals in society who see their existence and personality in opposing others, and they use all their skill in tuning the instrument of opposition. If everyone agrees, he disagrees, and if everyone disagrees, he agrees. He does not care about reasons and likes to always move contrary to others, and this attitude is evident in his personal movements, speech, and interactions. He tries to have unconventional and conspicuous behavior in how he grooms his hair and face, or the color and type of his clothing, or his tone and voice, or the use of words and terms, or his writing, and even in naming his child, thereby presenting himself as separate and better than others.

75) Tafsir Namūneh.

Messages

1- The greater the sanctity and grandeur, the more dangerous the negligence and the greater the reproach. «أفبهذا الحدیث انتم مدهنون»

2- Mudāhanah (compromise) and appeasement regarding the established principles of religion are unacceptable. «انّه لقرآن كریم... أفبهذا الحدیث انتم مدهنون»

3- Negligence, laxity, and compromise in religion gradually lead a person to denial. «أفبهذا الحدیث انتم مدهنون و تجعلون رزقكم انّكم تكذبون»

Ayahs

فَلَوْلَآ إِذَا بَلَغَتِ ٱلْحُلْقُومَ (۸۳)
So when it reaches the throat [of the dying person], (83)
وَأَنتُمْ حِينَئِذٍ تَنظُرُونَ (۸٤)
and at that moment you are looking on [at his bedside] (84)
وَنَحْنُ أَقْرَبُ إِلَيْهِ مِنكُمْ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا تُبْصِرُونَ (۸٥)
—and We are nearer to him than you are, though you do not perceive— (85)
فَلَوْلَآ إِن كُنتُمْ غَيْرَ مَدِينِينَ (۸٦)
then why do you not restore it, if you are not subject (86)
تَرْجِعُونَهَآ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۸۷)
[to Divine dispensation], if you are truthful? (87)

Key Points

If the deniers of the Resurrection doubt Allah’s power to resurrect the dead, do they deny His power to take the lives of humans? They see the scene of the dying person giving up his soul, where no one can do anything. They have witnessed their own helplessness and His display of power during the throes of death. That same power which can take the soul can return the soul. If death were not by divine will and decree, but merely accidental, it would be possible to find a remedy or prevent it.

Arguments must be presented to the deniers, just as Allah has repeatedly presented arguments in several verses of this Surah: «أفرأیتم ما تمنون», «أفرأیتم ما تحرثون», «أفرأیتم النار الّتى تورون». Of course, sometimes they must also be humbled by showing them the scene of their helplessness, just as Allah addresses them in these verses, saying: None of you has the power to ward off death or keep your loved one alive at the moment of death.

Allah is close to all servants, good or bad. «نحن اقرب الیه» What is important and valuable is that we draw near to God through faith and righteous deeds.

The word madīn (مَدین) comes from dīn (دین), meaning recompense, and thus Yawm ad-Dīn (Day of Recompense) is one of the names of the Day of Judgment: «مالك یوم الدین».

Looking is different from seeing. Often a person looks at something but does not see it, being preoccupied with something else. «تنظرون... لكن لا تبصرون»

Messages

1- The moment of death and the throes of death are certain for everyone. «اذا بلغت الحلقوم» (The word idhā [اذا] is used for events that are certain to occur.)

2- The departure of the soul from the body is through the respiratory passage and the throat (ḥulqūm). «بلغت الحلقوم»

3- To remove doubts about the Resurrection, Allah presents His power both in life and at the time of death, thereby completing the argument (itmām al-ḥujjah). «بلغت الحلقوم»

4- Allah is closer to man than the closest people around him. The closeness of others is physical, while His closeness results from His complete encompassment of the creation. «نحن اقرب الیه منكم»

5- The Resurrection and the recompense and reward in the Hereafter are certain. «ان كنتم غیر مدینین» (The use of the past tense verb to describe the future is a sign of its certainty and definitiveness.)

Ayahs

فَأَمَّآ إِن كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْمُقَرَّبِينَ (۸۸)
Then, if he be of those brought near, (88)
فَرَوْحٌ وَرَيْحَانٌ وَجَنَّتُ نَعِيمٍ (۸۹)
then ease, abundance, and a garden of bliss. (89)
وَأَمَّآ إِن كَانَ مِنْ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلْيَمِينِ (۹٠)
And if he be of the People of the Right Hand, (90)
فَسَلَـٰمٌ لَّكَ مِنْ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلْيَمِينِ (۹۱)
then [he will be told,] ‘Peace be on you, from the People of the Right Hand!’ (91)
وَأَمَّآ إِن كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْمُكَذِّبِينَ ٱلضَّآلِّينَ (۹۲)
But if he be of the impugners, the astray ones, (92)
فَنُزُلٌ مِّنْ حَمِيمٍ (۹۳)
then a treat of boiling water (93)
وَتَصْلِيَةُ جَحِيمٍ (۹٤)
and entry into hell. (94)
إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَهُوَ حَقُّ ٱلْيَقِينِ (۹٥)
Indeed this is certain truth. (95)
فَسَبِّحْ بِٱسْمِ رَبِّكَ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (۹٦)
So celebrate the Name of your Lord, the All-supreme! (96)

Key Points

"Rauh" (رَوح) means comfort, ease, and mercy. "Rayhan" (ریحان) refers to fragrant and perfumed plants; it has also been interpreted to mean sustenance and provision. "Tasliyah" (تصلیة) derives from "ṣalā" (صَلْى), meaning to taste, to be roasted, or to fall into.

The beginning of this Surah divided people into three groups: «كنتم ازواجاً ثلاثة» (you were three kinds): the Foremost (Mugarrabūn) in the Divine Presence, the Companions of the Right (Aṣḥāb al-Yamīn), and the Companions of the Left (Aṣḥāb ash-Shimāl). The end of the Surah also reiterates the destiny of these three groups.

Everyone receives a reward commensurate with their proximity (to Allah); as Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: If the dying person is among the Foremost, comfort (Rauh) and fragrance (Rayhan) in the grave and a Garden of Bliss (Jannat Na'īm) are decreed for his Hereafter. Of course, the "Mugarrabūn" (Foremost), according to verses 10 and 11, are those who are the pioneers in faith and righteous deeds. «و السابقون السابقون اولئك المقرّبون» (And the foremost are the foremost, those are the nearest [to Allah]).

At the moment of death, the Companions of the Right are only relieved of anxiety until they receive their reward on the Day of Resurrection. Apparently, the Companions of the Right are those whose record of deeds is given into their right hand, who are mentioned in verses 71 of Surah Al-Isra, 19 of Surah Al-Haqqah, and 7 of Surah Al-Inshiqaq with the phrase «من اوتى كتابه بیمینه» (who is given his record in his right hand).

The Companions of the Left are also those whose record of deeds is given into their left hand. It is narrated from Imam Baqir عليه السلام that the intent of «المكذّبین الضالّین» (the deniers, the astray) is the polytheists. (76)

Several meanings have been stated for the phrase «سلام لك من اصحاب الیمین» (Peace to you from the Companions of the Right): A) It is said to the people of Paradise (without a specific person being involved): Peace be upon you. B) The people of Paradise send greetings to the Prophet, because of his efforts that led them to this station. C) The Prophet is in goodness and safety because of the salvation of this group. (77)

In verse 51 we read: «ثمّ انّكم ایّها الضّالون المكذّبون، لأكلون من شجر من زقّوم» (Then indeed, O you who are astray [ḍāllūn] and deniers [mukadhdhibūn], you will surely eat from the trees of Zaqqūm). But in verse 92, He says: «و امّا ان كان من المكذّبین الضّالین فنزل من حمیم» (But if he is of the deniers [mukadhdhibīn], the astray [ḍāllīn], then his provision will be boiling water). Why is ḍāllīn (astray) placed before mukadhdhibīn (deniers) in the first verse, and reversed at the end?

Answer: It seems that the address in the first verse is in this world, where a person is led from deviation and being astray to denial. However, at the end of the Surah, the issue of giving up the soul is discussed, which is the beginning of the punishment, and punishment begins with the heavier transgression; denial is more significant than deviation.

In these verses, the moments of giving up the soul for the three groups are presented side-by-side so that man, in this spiritual exhibition, may choose which one: «روح و ریحان و جنّة نعیم» (comfort and fragrance and a Garden of Bliss)... «نزل من حمیم و تصلیة جحیم» (provision of boiling water and entry into Hellfire).

No reason is given for the reward of the Foremost and the Companions of the Right, but a reason is given for the punishment of the disbelievers: because they are deniers (mukadhdhib) and astray (ḍāll). Thus, chastisement requires a reason, but in grace and affection, no one seeks the cause. (78)

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The provision of boiling water is in the Barzakh (interspace) and the grave, and entry into Hell is on the Day of Resurrection. (79)

This Surah began with the mention of the Resurrection and concludes with the name of the Resurrection.

76) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 77) Tafsir Rahnama. 78) Tafsir Kabir Fakhr Razi. 79) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. The Foremost attain comfort and ease immediately after giving up the soul. «فروح و ریحان» (The letter 'fa' [ف] indicates the passage of a short time.)
  2. Death prepares the ground for three mercies for the Foremost: freedom from the sorrows and hardships of the world «فروحٌ» (comfort), attainment of Divine favors «ریحان» (fragrance/provision), and attainment of eternal fulfillment. «جنّة نعیم» (Garden of Bliss).
  3. We must believe and take seriously the moments of agony and death, and the reports concerning how the Foremost, the Companions of the Right, and the Companions of the Left give up their souls. «انّ هذا لهو حق الیقین» (Indeed, this is the absolute truth of certainty).
  4. The difficulty of the disbelievers giving up their souls and their punishment with boiling water (ḥamīm) and Hellfire (jaḥīm) is true and just. «فنزل من حمیم و تصلیة جحیم... انّ هذا لهو حق الیقین» (provision of boiling water and entry into Hellfire... Indeed, this is the absolute truth of certainty).
  5. The punishment for a lifetime of deviation and denial, «المكذّبین الضالّین» (the deniers, the astray), is provision with a scorching drink in a fiery abode, «فنزل من حمیم و تصلیة جحیم» (provision of boiling water and entry into Hellfire), and Allah is far removed from unjust punishment. «فسبح باسم ربّك...» (So glorify the Name of your Lord...).
  6. The existence of the Resurrection and the system of reward and punishment is a sign of Allah's Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) aimed at educating mankind, which necessitates the glorification of the Lord. «فسبّح باسم ربّك العظیم» (So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Great).
